analyses.name: Northern Trust Corporation
analyses.verdict: Clean new-high breakout in a rate-advantaged trust & custody franchise — a top-scoring, un-extended financial leader.

financials.summary: Trust, custody and wealth-management fee income plus net interest income on a large deposit base; higher-for-longer rates support spread income while equity markets lift assets under custody/administration (AUC/A) and assets under management. A capital-return story (buybacks + dividend) on top of steady fee growth.
highlights: Fee-driven, capital-light model with record AUC/A
Net interest income aided by elevated rates

accomplishments: Record custody/administration and wealth assets as markets rallied
Ongoing expense-discipline program to lift operating leverage

journey_52w.summary: Ground higher through the year with the rate tailwind and rising markets, then broke to a fresh 52-week high on a tight base — relative strength sitting right at the highs.

milestones.label: Mid-2025
milestones.note: Held up as rate-sensitive financials re-rated

milestones.label: Q2 2026 setup
milestones.note: Tight VCP base resolves to a new 52-week high, RS at highs

risks: Q2 earnings in ~9 days — event risk
Rate cuts would compress net interest income
watch: Holds the ~183.55 breakout on any pullback
RSI cooling from 72 without losing the base
critical.valuation: Reasonable for a capital-return-heavy trust bank; TradingView independently rates it BUY, confirming the technical breakout.

analyses.name: Crocs, Inc.
analyses.verdict: Fresh breakout with RSI still mid-range — a high-margin footwear compounder with room before overbought, and TV independently agrees (STRONG BUY).

financials.summary: The core Crocs brand keeps compounding on strong gross margins and cash generation, funding aggressive buybacks; the HEYDUDE acquisition remains the soft spot dragging the blended growth rate. Overall a cash-rich, shareholder-friendly model.
highlights: Gross margin in the high-50s% — premium for footwear
Strong free cash flow funding share buybacks
Crocs brand growth offsetting HEYDUDE softness

accomplishments: Crocs-brand market-share gains and international expansion

journey_52w.summary: Based for much of the year as HEYDUDE worries capped the multiple, then momentum reasserted (+33% over 3 months) into a clean new-high breakout with RSI only 59.

milestones.label: Late 2025
milestones.note: Sentiment weighed down by HEYDUDE growth concerns

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: Breakout to new highs, +33% 3-mo momentum, TV STRONG BUY

risks: Discretionary-consumer sensitivity in a risk-off tape
Fashion/fad cyclicality of the core clog
watch: RSI has room from 59 — watch it doesn't spike too fast
critical.valuation: Inexpensive on earnings for the margin/FCF profile; TV STRONG BUY confirms the setup.

analyses.name: Arista Networks, Inc.
analyses.verdict: The cleanest large-cap AI-networking breakout — new high with RSI only 59, direct leverage to the data-center buildout.

financials.summary: High-performance data-center switching/routing with best-in-class software (EOS) and gross margins in the low-to-mid 60s%. Revenue growth in the high-teens/20s% driven by cloud and AI back-end networking, with very strong operating margins and a fortress balance sheet (net cash, no debt).

accomplishments: Positioned as a primary beneficiary of AI back-end (Ethernet) networking
Continued share gains in high-speed data-center switching

journey_52w.summary: Rode the AI-networking narrative higher with intermittent capex-timing wobbles, then resolved a base to new highs on RSI 59 — un-extended leadership.

milestones.note: AI/Ethernet networking demand narrative builds

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: New-high breakout, RSI 59, RS at highs

risks: Hyperscaler capex is lumpy quarter to quarter
Customer concentration among a few cloud buyers
critical.valuation: Premium multiple justified by growth + margins; TradingView independently rates it BUY.

analyses.name: Monster Beverage Corporation
analyses.verdict: Defensive-consumer leader at new highs out of a tight base — the kind of low-beta ballast that works on a jittery open.

financials.summary: The #2 global energy-drink brand with premium gross margins, pricing power, and a Coca-Cola distribution partnership. Steady mid-single to high-single-digit revenue growth, strong cash generation, and a clean balance sheet.
highlights: Gross margin recovering toward the mid-50s%

journey_52w.summary: Recovered from a margin-pressure period as pricing flowed through, grinding to a fresh 52-week high on a tight VCP base with RSI 72.

milestones.note: Margins rebuild as input costs ease and pricing sticks

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: At new highs, tight base, RS at highs

critical.valuation: Premium staple multiple; TradingView independently rates it BUY.

analyses.name: Liquidia Corporation
analyses.verdict: The momentum standout (+102% 3-mo) on YUTREPIA commercialization — highest reward, highest risk; keep it a smaller line.

financials.summary: A commercial-stage biopharma centered on treprostinil-based therapies for pulmonary arterial hypertension (PAH) and PH-ILD. Revenue is still early-stage and the company is not yet consistently profitable — the story is the YUTREPIA (inhaled treprostinil) launch ramp, not current earnings. Balance-sheet runway and litigation outcomes matter more than a single fiscal year's P&L.
highlights: YUTREPIA approval unlocks the PAH/PH-ILD opportunity
Cash runway is a key line to watch
Value is in the commercial ramp, not trailing numbers

accomplishments: YUTREPIA (treprostinil inhalation) FDA approval and launch

journey_52w.summary: Re-rated hard over the past year as regulatory and litigation overhangs cleared toward the YUTREPIA approval, driving a +102% three-month move into a breakout with RSI still only 62.

milestones.note: Regulatory/legal path toward YUTREPIA clears

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: +102% 3-mo momentum, breakout with room (RSI 62)

risks: Cash burn during launch; earnings ~30 days out
watch: Quarterly launch metrics and cash runway
critical.valuation: A launch-ramp call, not an earnings multiple; TradingView independently rates it BUY, which is unusual confirmation for a name this small.

analyses.name: Eli Lilly and Company
analyses.verdict: Mega-cap GLP-1 leader basing tightly near highs — a secular grower you can own through the noise; size for the high share price.

financials.summary: Explosive growth led by the incretin franchise — Mounjaro (diabetes) and Zepbound (obesity) — layered on an oncology/immunology/neuroscience pipeline. Revenue grew sharply YoY with strong operating leverage; the constraint has been manufacturing capacity, which Lilly is expanding aggressively.
highlights: Heavy capacity capex to meet GLP-1 demand

accomplishments: Pipeline read-outs across incretin and neuro

journey_52w.summary: A volatile but upward year as the GLP-1 demand/supply story dominated; the stock is now basing just ~4% off its high with RSI 65 — coiled rather than extended.

milestones.note: GLP-1 demand outstrips supply; capacity buildout accelerates

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: Tight base near highs, RSI 65, +27% 3-mo momentum

risks: Very high absolute share price — position-size carefully
catalysts: Capacity coming online to unlock demand
critical.valuation: Premium multiple, but underpinned by one of the largest growth ramps in pharma; TradingView independently rates it BUY.

analyses.name: NVIDIA Corporation
analyses.verdict: The AI bellwether reset ~10% off its high with RSI back to 50 — an un-extended re-entry, and TV independently rates it STRONG BUY. A core held name.

financials.summary: The defining AI-compute franchise: data-center GPUs (Hopper→Blackwell) drove a roughly doubling of revenue with gross margins around 75%. Enormous free cash flow, net cash, and a networking (InfiniBand/NVLink) plus software (CUDA) moat around the silicon.

accomplishments: Networking + CUDA software lock-in extending the moat

journey_52w.summary: Led the AI complex higher, then pulled back ~10% into a base as the group consolidated — RSI reset to 50, which historically has been a re-accumulation zone rather than a top.

milestones.note: Blackwell ramp; AI capex cycle broadens

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: ~10% pullback, RSI 50 reset, TV STRONG BUY

risks: AI-capex digestion fears in the group
Highest-weight name — it moves the whole tape
watch: Holds the ~211 base / reclaims the high
critical.valuation: Not cheap on headline multiples but reasonable on forward growth; TradingView independently rates it STRONG BUY, aligning with the reset thesis.

analyses.name: Fortinet, Inc.
analyses.verdict: Cyber compounder basing near highs on +105% 3-mo momentum and strong FCF — a held name that's also a top setup. TV: BUY.

financials.summary: A profitable cybersecurity vendor with a hardware (FortiGate) + software/services model, high recurring service revenue, industry-leading operating margins and prodigious free cash flow. Growth is shifting toward SASE/security services as the firewall base matures.
highlights: Operating margins among the best in security software

accomplishments: SASE and security-operations expansion beyond firewalls
Large installed base driving refresh + upsell

journey_52w.summary: Re-rated over the past year (+105% 3-mo) as the firewall-refresh cycle and services growth reaccelerated; now basing just ~4% off its high.

milestones.note: Refresh cycle + services growth reaccelerate

milestones.label: Q2 2026
milestones.note: +105% 3-mo momentum, tight base near highs

critical.valuation: Reasonable for the margin/FCF profile; TradingView independently rates it BUY.

analyses.name: Targa Resources, Inc.
analyses.verdict: The timely energy pick — a midstream breakout with Permian NGL growth, catching an added bid as Iran/Hormuz lifts oil. TV: BUY.

financials.summary: A leading Permian-focused midstream gatherer/processor with growing NGL logistics and export volumes. Record throughput drove higher adjusted EBITDA; the model is fee-based volume growth plus rising capital returns (dividend hikes + buybacks) as major projects come online.
highlights: Rising dividend + buybacks as projects de-lever

journey_52w.summary: Trended higher on volume growth and capital-return momentum; now near its highs (RSI 62) with the geopolitical oil bid as an added near-term tailwind.

milestones.note: Volume growth + returns lift the stock

milestones.label: Jul 2026
milestones.note: Near highs; Hormuz/oil premium adds a catalyst

risks: Volume/commodity sensitivity if oil reverses on de-escalation
catalysts: Oil/NGL price strength on Middle-East risk
critical.valuation: Fair on EV/EBITDA with a growing yield; TradingView independently rates it BUY.

analyses.name: Apple Inc.
analyses.verdict: At a fresh high with RS at highs — the most widely held name across the book, working cleanly. TV: BUY.

financials.summary: Record Services revenue (App Store, cloud, payments, subscriptions) at high gross margin remains the growth and margin engine, layered on the iPhone/Mac/iPad hardware base. Enormous free cash flow funds one of the largest buyback + dividend programs in the market; overall gross margin sits in the high-40s%.
highlights: Services at a record, gross margin near ~70% for Services
Company gross margin in the high-40s%

accomplishments: Latest iPhone cycle sustaining the installed base
Continued Apple-silicon transition across the Mac line

journey_52w.summary: Digested China-demand and AI-positioning worries earlier in the year, then pushed back to new highs — sitting just 0.3% off its high with relative strength at the highs.

milestones.note: China-demand and AI-feature debates weigh, then resolve

milestones.label: Q3 2026
milestones.note: New 52-week high, RS at highs

catalysts: Next iPhone cycle + AI feature rollout
critical.valuation: Premium multiple for quality/FCF; TradingView independently rates it BUY.

analyses.name: SpaceX (recent listing)
analyses.verdict: NEW LISTING (~19 trading days) — trade the thesis, not the quant signals: dominant launch franchise + Starlink scaling, but a rich valuation and post-IPO volatility. No TradingView rating yet — that's expected.

financials.summary: As a brand-new listing the public financial history is thin and the breakout/RS/VCP signals are blank until ~2-3 months of trading accumulate. The investment case is the business, not a fiscal-year P&L: an unmatched global launch cadence (reusable Falcon + Starship development) and Starlink — a fast-scaling satellite-broadband subscription business that is the real revenue/valuation engine. Treat any early numbers as incomplete.
highlights: New listing — quant signals blank until more history accrues
Starlink subscriber growth is the key financial driver
Valuation is rich and priced on future scale

accomplishments: Record launch cadence with reusable boosters
Starship development progressing toward heavier-lift capability

journey_52w.summary: Only days of public trading: an IPO pop followed by a pullback (currently ~31% below its brief post-listing high on light history). Price discovery is still underway — expect wide swings.

milestones.label: Recent
milestones.note: Public debut; initial pop then give-back

milestones.label: Now
milestones.note: ~19 trading days of history; signals not yet meaningful

risks: Extreme post-IPO volatility and thin trading history
Valuation priced for near-perfect Starlink/Starship execution
Lock-up/float dynamics of a fresh listing
catalysts: First public financial disclosures as a listed company
watch: Where price discovery settles as history builds
critical.valuation: Thesis-driven and rich — own it as a small, high-conviction growth position sized for volatility, not as a quant breakout. Educational, not financial advice.
